sections.label: Compose File
sections.aiHint: Top-level metadata for the docker-compose.yml file.

fields.label: Compose file version
fields.help: 3.9 is the latest stable version and supports all current features.
fields.aiHint: The Docker Compose file format version. 3.9 is recommended.

fields.label: Project name
fields.help: Used as the prefix for container names and networks. Defaults to the directory name if omitted.
fields.aiHint: Name of the Compose project. Used as prefix for all resource names.

sections.label: Service
sections.aiHint: Defines a containerized service. Each service corresponds to one container or a set of identical containers.

fields.label: Service name
fields.help: Used as the DNS hostname within the Compose network — other services reach this service by this name.
fields.aiHint: Name of the service e.g. app, api, frontend, backend, db, redis, nginx.

fields.label: Image
fields.help: Used when pulling from a registry. If 'Build context' is set, image acts as the tag for the built image.
fields.aiHint: Docker image to use e.g. postgres:16, redis:7-alpine, nginx:1.25. Leave blank if building from source.

fields.label: Build context
fields.help: Path to the directory containing the Dockerfile. Use when building from source.
fields.aiHint: Path to the directory containing the Dockerfile e.g. ., ./frontend, ./backend.

fields.label: Dockerfile path
fields.help: Path to Dockerfile relative to the build context. Leave blank to use 'Dockerfile' in the context root.
fields.aiHint: Relative path to the Dockerfile. Leave blank for default Dockerfile.

fields.label: Container name
fields.help: Explicit container name. If omitted, Docker generates one from the project and service name.
fields.aiHint: Explicit container name. Leave blank to auto-generate.

fields.label: Port mappings (one per line)
fields.help: Format: HOST_PORT:CONTAINER_PORT. Use 127.0.0.1:3000:3000 to bind only to localhost.
fields.aiHint: Port mappings in HOST:CONTAINER format e.g. 3000:3000, 5432:5432, 80:8080.

fields.label: Environment variables (one per line)
fields.help: Use KEY=VALUE format. For sensitive values use .env file or Docker secrets instead.
fields.aiHint: Environment variables in KEY=VALUE format for this service.

fields.label: Env file path
fields.help: Path to a .env file to load variables from. Relative to the Compose file.
fields.aiHint: Path to .env file for loading environment variables e.g. .env, ./config/.env.

fields.label: Volume mounts (one per line)
fields.help: Format: VOLUME_NAME:CONTAINER_PATH or ./HOST_PATH:CONTAINER_PATH. Named volumes are managed by Docker.
fields.aiHint: Volume mounts. Use named_volume:/path for persistence or ./local:/container for dev hot reload.

fields.label: Depends on (one per line)
fields.help: Service names this service starts after. Does not wait for the dependency to be ready — use healthchecks for that.
fields.aiHint: Services that must start before this one e.g. db, redis, message-broker.

fields.label: Networks (one per line)
fields.help: Networks this service joins. Services on the same network can communicate by service name.
fields.aiHint: Named networks this service should join. Services on the same network can communicate by name.

fields.label: Restart policy
fields.help: unless-stopped is recommended for most services — restarts after crashes but not after docker stop.
fields.aiHint: When to restart the container. unless-stopped is recommended for production services.

fields.label: CPU limit
fields.help: Maximum CPU cores this service may use. 0.5 = half a core. Requires Compose v3 with deploy settings.
fields.aiHint: Maximum CPU cores e.g. 0.5 for half a core, 1.0 for one full core.

fields.label: Memory limit
fields.help: Maximum memory. Format: 512m, 1g. Prevents a service from consuming all host memory.
fields.aiHint: Maximum memory e.g. 256m, 512m, 1g.

fields.label: Memory reservation
fields.help: Soft limit — Docker tries to keep memory below this but allows bursting to the limit.
fields.aiHint: Soft memory reservation. Should be less than the memory limit.

fields.label: Healthcheck command
fields.help: Used with depends_on condition: service_healthy. Makes other services wait for this one to be ready.
fields.aiHint: Command to verify the service is healthy e.g. pg_isready -U postgres, curl -f http://localhost/health.

fields.label: Healthcheck interval
fields.aiHint: How often to run the healthcheck.

fields.label: Healthcheck retries
fields.aiHint: Number of consecutive failures before marking the service unhealthy.

fields.label: Command override
fields.help: Overrides the CMD from the Dockerfile.
fields.aiHint: Override the Dockerfile CMD e.g. npm run start:prod, python manage.py runserver.

fields.label: User
fields.help: Runs the container as a specific UID:GID. Overrides the USER in the Dockerfile.
fields.aiHint: UID:GID to run the container as for security e.g. 1001:1001.

sections.label: Named Volume
sections.aiHint: Declares named volumes for persistent data storage managed by Docker.

fields.label: Volume name
fields.help: Named volumes persist data between container restarts and recreations.
fields.aiHint: Name of the Docker volume e.g. postgres_data, redis_data, uploads.

fields.label: Driver
fields.help: local is the default. Use other drivers for NFS, cloud storage, etc.
fields.aiHint: Volume driver. local is standard. Use nfs or cloud-specific drivers for shared storage.

fields.label: External volume
fields.help: If true, the volume must be created manually before running Compose. Compose will not create it.
fields.aiHint: Whether this volume is managed externally and must exist before running Compose.

sections.label: Named Network
sections.aiHint: Declares named networks for controlling service-to-service communication and isolation.

fields.label: Network name
fields.help: Services on the same network can reach each other by service name.
fields.aiHint: Name of the network e.g. frontend, backend, database.

fields.label: Driver
fields.help: bridge is correct for single-host Compose. overlay is for Docker Swarm multi-host.
fields.aiHint: Network driver. bridge is standard for single-host setups.

fields.label: External network
fields.help: If true, the network must be created manually before running Compose.
fields.aiHint: Whether this network is managed externally and must exist before running Compose.

fields.label: Subnet (CIDR)
fields.help: Optional custom subnet. Leave blank to let Docker auto-assign.
fields.aiHint: Custom subnet CIDR for the network e.g. 172.20.0.0/16. Leave blank for auto-assignment.
